content.thinking:
Now I understand the setup. There's an Elasticsearch cluster running in the `elasticsearch` namespace with:
1. `ops-user` with `superuser` role - working
2. `report-user` with `viewer` role - cannot authenticate (newly added)

The issue seems to be that the file realm secrets for the two users are in separate secrets:
- `es-file-realm-aggregate` - contains ops-user credentials
- `user-provided-file-realm` - contains report-user credentials

The Elasticsearch cluster is likely only configured to use `es-file-realm-aggregate` for the file realm, not `user-provided-file-realm`. 

The `report-user` can't authenticate because their credentials aren't being loaded from the active file realm. Let me look at the case resources to see how everything's configured.

file.content:
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: es-cluster
  namespace: elasticsearch
spec:
  serviceName: es-cluster
  podManagementPolicy: Parallel
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: es-cluster
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: es-cluster
    spec:
      containers:
        - name: elasticsearch
          image: docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch:7.17.9
          env:
            - name: POD_NAME
              valueFrom:
                fieldRef:
                  fieldPath: metadata.name
            - name: ES_JAVA_OPTS
              value: "-Xms512m -Xmx512m"
            - name: ELASTIC_PASSWORD
              value: "elasticpass"
          ports:
            - containerPort: 9200
              name: http
            - containerPort: 9300
              name: transport
          readinessProbe:
            tcpSocket:
              port: 9200
            initialDelaySeconds: 10
            periodSeconds: 5
            timeoutSeconds: 1
            failureThreshold: 6
          volumeMounts:
            - name: es-config
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/elasticsearch.yml
              subPath: elasticsearch.yml
            - name: data
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/data
            - name: http-certs
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/http-certs
              readOnly: true
            - name: file-realm
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/users
              subPath: users
              readOnly: true
            - name: file-realm
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/users_roles
              subPath: users_roles
              readOnly: true
            - name: file-realm
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/roles.yml
              subPath: roles.yml
              readOnly: true
          resources:
            requests:
              cpu: "500m"
              memory: "1Gi"
            limits:
              cpu: "1"
              memory: "2Gi"
      volumes:
        - name: es-config
          configMap:
            name: es-config
            items:
              - key: elasticsearch.yml
                path: elasticsearch.yml
        - name: http-certs
          secret:
            secretName: es-http-tls
        - name: file-realm
          secret:
            secretName: es-file-realm-aggregate
            items:
              - key: users
                path: users
              - key: users_roles
                path: users_roles
              - key: roles.yml
                path: roles.yml
  volumeClaimTemplates:
    - metadata:
        name: data
      spec:
        accessModes: ["ReadWriteOnce"]
        resources:
          requests:
            storage: 5Gi

Now I understand the full picture:

1. The statefulset mounts the `es-file-realm-aggregate` secret as the file realm.
2. `es-file-realm-aggregate` only has `ops-user` with `superuser` role.
3. `user-provided-file-realm` has `report-user` with `viewer` role but is NOT mounted anywhere.

The fix is to merge `user-provided-file-realm` into `es-file-realm-aggregate` so both users are in the same secret. The secret is mounted as a volume and Kubernetes will hot-reload it (within kubelet sync period, usually ~1-2 min), so the pods don't need to restart.
Elasticsearch does watch the file realm files and reloads them automatically when they change, and Kubernetes secrets mounted as volumes update automatically through the kubelet's sync cycle. So my plan is to decode both secrets' user files, merge them together, update `es-file-realm-aggregate` with the combined content, and then verify that `report-user` can authenticate.

content.text: Now I understand the issue. The statefulset mounts `es-file-realm-aggregate` (which only has `ops-user`) as the file realm. The `report-user` credentials exist in `user-provided-file-realm` but that secret is never mounted. I need to merge both secrets into `es-file-realm-aggregate`.

input.command:
# Decode both secrets' users and users_roles
echo "=== es-file-realm-aggregate ==="
kubectl -n elasticsearch get secret es-file-realm-aggregate -o jsonpath='{.data.users}' | base64 -d
echo "---"
kubectl -n elasticsearch get secret es-file-realm-aggregate -o jsonpath='{.data.users_roles}' | base64 -d

echo "=== user-provided-file-realm ==="
kubectl -n elasticsearch get secret user-provided-file-realm -o jsonpath='{.data.users}' | base64 -d
echo "---"
kubectl -n elasticsearch get secret user-provided-file-realm -o jsonpath='{.data.users_roles}' | base64 -d

# Merge the two users files and users_roles files
MERGED_USERS=$(printf '%s\n%s' \
  "ops-user:\$2a\$10\$lhpWC60QIoxEvShJftlj2OGqCdUa0Rgta5VX0eHGBBNJo56hI3zBu" \
  "report-user:\$2a\$10\$c73GXeBDP5naxuLo.UWYdOelKrLq0zCfg/Ptn766JtaRmCbhWBmR.")

MERGED_USERS_ROLES=$(printf '%s\n%s' \
  "superuser:ops-user" \
  "viewer:report-user")

echo "=== Merged users ==="
echo "$MERGED_USERS"
echo "=== Merged users_roles ==="
echo "$MERGED_USERS_ROLES"

# Patch the aggregate secret with merged content
kubectl -n elasticsearch patch secret es-file-realm-aggregate \
  --type='json' \
  -p="[
    {\"op\": \"replace\", \"path\": \"/data/users\", \"value\": \"$(echo "$MERGED_USERS" | base64 -w0)\"},
    {\"op\": \"replace\", \"path\": \"/data/users_roles\", \"value\": \"$(echo "$MERGED_USERS_ROLES" | base64 -w0)\"}
  ]"
